stimulus: Subscription meal-kit services are giving home cooks more options to receive ready-to-prepare ingredients than ever before. Whether ordering kits for weeknight dinners, quick lunches, or weekend recipes, customers can choose meals that match their schedules and tastes. Companies profit too: studies have shown that subscribers are willing to pay a premium for the convenience of pre-portioned ingredients. Still, it can be hard for companies to keep meal kits both fresh and affordable, as standard food-distribution systems may not handle small, customized orders efficiently.
stem: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?

answerOptions.content: It describes a company's recent success with new products and then explains multiple factors that may have contributed.
It presents two contrasting product-marketing techniques and then provides examples of one of those techniques.
It discusses several recent innovations in food preparation and then suggests some potential applications.
It introduces a trend in consumer products and then explains how the trend both benefits and poses a challenge to companies.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The text introduces the trend of subscription meal kits, explains that the trend benefits companies (subscribers pay a premium), and then explains a challenge (keeping kits fresh and affordable using standard distribution).

Choice A is incorrect. The text doesn't focus on a particular company. Choice B is incorrect. The text doesn't present contrasting marketing techniques. Choice C is incorrect. The text doesn't describe recent innovations in food preparation or potential applications.
